title: Use shorter TTL for null item-registry cache entries
description:
## Evidence
`Agent.Memory/MemorySmithItemRegistry.GetAsync()` caches null results with the same TTL as successful results (`_cache[slug] = (spec, ...)` stores `spec=null` with full TTL).

## Impact
A transient gateway failure or a newly published page can be treated as absent until the TTL expires. A miss becomes sticky.

## Fix
Options: (a) use much shorter TTL for null entries (e.g. 5s vs 60s), (b) don't cache null at all, or (c) cache null only for clearly "not found" responses, not transport failures.

## Verification
Verified against codebase 2026-06-23. See `Data/Pages/audits/msa-audit-verification-6-23.md` section F6.
